title: Ansible Segment 13
summary: A focused development segment of Ansible core covering 95 PRs across 5 dependency layers. This segment emphasizes internal architecture modernization (the new lib/ansible/_internal/ subsystem for templating, powershell, YAML, error handling, and RPC), module bug fixes (user, apt, dnf, find, file, deb822_repository), executor and strategy improvements, connection plugin enhancements (winrm, psrp, ssh), Galaxy CLI and collection management updates, and ansible-test infrastructure maintenance.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: A focused development segment of Ansible core covering 95 PRs across 5 dependency layers. This segment emphasizes internal architecture modernization (the new lib/ansible/_internal/ subsystem for templating, powershell, YAML, error handling, and RPC), module bug fixes (user, apt, dnf, find, file, deb822_repository), executor and strategy improvements, connection plugin enhancements (winrm, psrp, ssh), Galaxy CLI and collection management updates, and ansible-test infrastructure maintenance.

nodes.label: Ansible Modules
nodes.description: Core modules with concentrated changes: user.py (10 edge references, 5 PRs across layers 0-4), apt.py (3 PRs), dnf.py (3 PRs), deb822_repository.py (3 PRs), find.py, file.py, apt_key.py. Fixes cover authentication, package state handling, and repository management.

nodes.label: Task Executor & Process Management
nodes.description: Task execution core: task_executor.py (3 edge references), module_common.py, task_queue_manager.py, task_result.py, process/worker.py, and powershell bootstrap scripts. Handles task dispatch and result aggregation.

nodes.label: Internal Architecture (New)
nodes.description: Newly introduced internal subsystem providing modernized implementations: _templating/ (jinja_bits, jinja_common, engine, template_vars), _powershell/ (clixml, script), _yaml/ (loader, dumper), _errors/ (captured, task_timeout, attribute_unavailable), _worker/, _rpc_host, _ansiballz, _task, and _plugins.

nodes.label: Module Utilities
nodes.description: Shared module support libraries including csharp/Ansible.Basic.cs, powershell utilities, and common helpers. Provides cross-platform module infrastructure.

nodes.label: Connection Plugins
nodes.description: Connection layer: winrm.py, psrp.py, ssh.py, local.py, and base __init__.py. PRs improve Windows remote management, SSH transport, and connection lifecycle handling.

nodes.label: Plugin Framework (Misc)
nodes.description: Plugin loader, inventory plugins, callback plugins, shell plugins, and plugin infrastructure. Includes validate-modules sanity tooling and pylint plugin updates.

nodes.label: Strategy Plugins
nodes.description: Execution strategy __init__.py and free.py. Controls play execution ordering, task batching, and host failure handling during playbook runs.

nodes.label: Action Plugins
nodes.description: Action plugin base class (6 edge references, 4 PRs spanning layers 0-3) and specific action plugins. Mediates between executor, connection, and module layers for task execution.

nodes.label: Galaxy & Collection Management
nodes.description: Galaxy API communication, collection install/verification, galaxy_api_proxy, dependency_resolution, GPG signature verification, and concrete artifact manager. CLI galaxy commands drive this subsystem.

nodes.label: Playbook Engine
nodes.description: Playbook data structures, play/task/block/role representations, and attribute system. Provides the structural foundation for execution.

nodes.label: CI/CD & Test Infrastructure
nodes.description: Azure Pipelines configuration, test container management (docker_util.py with 3 edge references), ansible-test container integration, and codecov publishing scripts.

nodes.label: Variable Management
nodes.description: Variable manager (3 edge references spanning layers 0-2) for variable precedence, merging, and resolution during playbook execution.

nodes.label: Core Utilities
nodes.description: Display system, sentinel, unsafe proxy, and other shared utilities used across the codebase for output formatting and safety.

nodes.label: Filter Plugins
nodes.description: Jinja2 filter plugins: core.py filter implementations and supporting filter documentation. Provides template-accessible data transformations.

nodes.label: Lookup Plugins
nodes.description: Lookup plugins for data retrieval during template evaluation. Provides external data access within Jinja2 expressions.

nodes.label: CLI Tools
nodes.description: Command-line interfaces: galaxy.py (3 edge references), adhoc.py, and console.py. Entry points for ansible-galaxy collection operations.

edges.label: modules import shared utilities from module_utils
executor uses internal templating, worker, and RPC subsystems
executor invokes action plugins for task dispatch
action plugins use connection plugins for remote execution
strategy plugins orchestrate executor task runs
executor resolves variables through var manager
module_utils leverages internal powershell and clixml
ansible-galaxy CLI drives galaxy collection operations
strategy reads playbook structure for execution order
internal templating engine loads filter plugins
internal templating engine invokes lookup plugins
CI infrastructure runs ansible-test with plugin validation
